rationale: Graphic Designer at Infosys with a marketing manager background and only casual ChatGPT experimentation; skills are CI/CD (8mo), Sales, and Illustrator — zero ML, IR, or engineering depth relevant to this role.

QA Engineer with only 1.9 years of experience, currently at fictional company Initech (honeypot signal), with web/frontend background and only a small self-built RAG side project — no production IR or ranking experience.

9.4yr career spans DevOps at CRED, Java backend at Cognizant (IT services), and infrastructure at Wayne Enterprises (fictional company); MLflow appears as an advanced skill but there is no production embeddings, retrieval, or ranking experience.

Business Analyst with 1.9 years in operations/logistics management at Acme Corp with no engineering background, citing only online courses on RAG and LangChain experimentation — fundamentally non-technical profile.

Java Developer whose current company is fictional Dunder Mifflin (honeypot signal) and prior role is at TCS (IT services); top skills are Project Management, HTML, and Excel at beginner level with no ML or IR experience whatsoever.

Project Manager with a career entirely in SEO content writing, marketing leadership at Acme Corp, and a Business Analyst role at fictional Stark Industries (honeypot signal) — no engineering or ML work of any kind.

HR Manager with career at Wipro (IT services) in brand design and at fictional Dunder Mifflin in sales/accounting; FAISS listed as an advanced skill (13mo) is implausible keyword inflation on a non-technical HR/sales profile.

Java Developer with 7.5yr career split across Wipro (IT services Java backend), Acme Corp (QA), and CRED (DevOps); primary ML skill is Speech Recognition advanced (53mo) — wrong domain for IR/ranking — with no retrieval or NLP production work.

6.7yr engineer with NLP advanced (31mo) and product-company experience at CRED and Flipkart, but primary AI skill is ASR (speech recognition, wrong domain) and career history shows no production IR, retrieval, or ranking systems.

6.7yr software engineer with Learning to Rank listed as an advanced skill (49mo, 59 endorsements) which is directly relevant, but the entire career — HCL, TCS, Mphasis — is at IT-services firms with no product-company ranking/retrieval production evidence.
